title: CCPA/CPRA ‚Äî Dark Patterns ‚Äî Pre-Checked ‚ÄúFinancial Incentive‚Äù Enrollment on Account Settings Save

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA ¬ß1798.125 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA ¬ß1798.125 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.4.20: CCPA/CPRA ‚Äî Dark Patterns ‚Äî Pre-Checked ‚ÄúFinancial Incentive‚Äù Enrollment on Account Settings Save.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.20' running on sourcetype profile:update and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=app sourcetype="profile:update" field_changed="marketing_incentive_optin" new_value=true earliest=-30d
| join type=left user_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="ux:form_submit" form_id="financial_incentive_optin" earliest=-30d
    | stats min(_time) as first_submit by user_id
  ]
| where isnull(first_submit) OR first_submit > _time
| table user_id, _time, new_value, first_submit
description: Financial incentive programs require opt-in consent. Server logs showing `marketing_incentive_optin` toggled true without a distinct affirmative action event (checkbox submit) support detection of prohibited default-on patterns.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app, web and sourcetypes profile:update, ux:form_submit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require `client_event_id` correlation if available‚Äîstrengthen join; (2) privacy counsel defines valid proof events; (3) auto-create remediation tickets; (4) exclude internal test accounts; (5) align with ¬ß1798.125 documentation. Indexes required: `index=app`, `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=profile:update`, `sourcetype=ux:form_submit`.
visualization: Table (users), Single value (count), Timeline. Key fields: `first_submit`, `new_value`, `user_id`.
knownFalsePositives: CCPA program noise during annual evidence pulls, GRC data refreshes, and vendor-driven reporting template changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Application audit logs via HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=app` `sourcetype="profile:update"` (user_id, field_changed, old_value, new_value, client_event_id, _time); `index=web` `sourcetype="ux:form_submit"` (user_id, form_id, _time).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Require `client_event_id` correlation if available‚Äîstrengthen join; (2) privacy counsel defines valid proof events; (3) auto-create remediation tickets; (4) exclude internal test accounts; (5) align with ¬ß1798.125 documentation.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app sourcetype="profile:update" field_changed="marketing_incentive_optin" new_value=true earliest=-30d
| join type=left user_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="ux:form_submit" form_id="financial_incentive_optin" earliest=-30d
    | stats min(_time) as first_submit by user_id
  ]
| where isnull(first_submit) OR first_submit > _time
| table user_id, _time, new_value, first_submit
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA ‚Äî Dark Patterns ‚Äî Pre-Checked ‚ÄúFinancial Incentive‚Äù Enrollment on Account Settings Save** ‚Äî Financial incentive programs require opt-in consent. Server logs showing `marketing_incentive_optin` toggled true without a distinct affirmative action event (checkbox submit) support detection of prohibited default-on patterns.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `sourcetype="profile:update"` (user_id, field_changed, old_value, new_value, client_event_id, _time); `index=web` `sourcetype="ux:form_submit"` (user_id, form_id, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Application audit logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app; **sourcetype**: profile:update. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=app, sourcetype="profile:update", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(first_submit) OR first_submit > _time` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, first_submit, new_value, user_id.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (users), Single value (count), Timeline.
